name: Review Request Engine

parameters.content:
## Review Request Engine

### How it works

1. The workflow triggers when a legal matter is closed and parses the matter details.
2. It verifies the closure validity and prepares a review request to be sent after a 48-hour delay.
3. Upon delay completion, it checks compliance and sends a rating request SMS if approved.
4. The workflow also handles incoming review replies, validating ratings and branching based on client satisfaction.
5. Happy clients are sent a Google review link if authorized, while unhappy clients receive a feedback form SMS; firms are notified accordingly.
6. Throughout, non-compliant or opted-out cases are gracefully skipped with no operation nodes.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Configure webhook URLs in the external systems to trigger the 'When Matter Closed' and 'When Review Reply Received' webhook nodes.
- [ ] Set up n8n Variables for firm details that the 'Prepare Review Request' and 'Parse Client Reply' nodes reference.
- [ ] Ensure the Twilio credentials are configured in n8n for sending SMS messages.
- [ ] Configure email credentials in n8n for the 'Inform Firm Request Sent' and 'Inform Firm of Unhappy Client' email nodes.
- [ ] Verify the sub-workflows used for compliance checks are imported and active in the n8n instance.

### Customization

Adjust timing in the 'Wait 48 Hours' node as needed to control the delay before sending review requests. Update compliance sub-workflows and message contents to fit firm policies.

## Matter closure trigger and validation

Initial webhook trigger on matter closure, parsing of matter details, validation of closure, and preparation for review request setup.

## Delay before review request

Wait node enforcing a 48-hour delay before proceeding with review requests.

## Compliance and approval for rating request

Sub-workflow to check review request compliance, and conditional branch to approve or skip sending rating request SMS.

## Send rating request SMS and notify firm

Sending the rating request SMS via Twilio and notifying the firm by email when the request is sent.

## Review reply trigger and parsing

Webhook to receive review replies from clients and parsing of the reply data for processing.

## Validate rating and satisfaction check

Conditionally checks if the review reply is a valid rating, and determines client satisfaction to branch further.

## Happy client review handling

Sub-workflow for compliance check, approval branch, sending Google review link SMS, and skipping opted-out recipients with 4-5 star ratings.

## Unhappy client feedback handling

Sub-workflow for compliance check, approval branch, sending feedback form SMS, and skipping opted-out recipients with 1-3 star ratings, along with firm notification.

nodes.name: When Matter Closure Requested
nodes.notes:
Receives the matter-close event from your practice management system.

Copy the Production URL and paste it into your practice management system's webhook settings. Configure it to fire on 'matter closed' or 'matter updated to Closed status'.

Clio: Settings → Webhooks → New Webhook → Topic: Matter → paste this URL → subscribe to 'matter.updated' events. The Read Matter Details step detects status = 'Closed' automatically and ignores all other updates.

Generic: POST JSON with 'matter_status: closed' (or 'event_type: matter.closed'), 'client_phone', 'client_name', and 'matter_title'. See deploy guide for full field list.

Required fields:
  matter_id — your internal matter reference
  matter_title — the case name for the firm notification
  client_name — used to personalise the SMS
  client_phone — the number to text (required; without it the event is skipped)
  matter_status or status — must equal 'closed' or 'Closed'

nodes.name: Parse Matter Closure Details
nodes.notes:
Reads the incoming webhook and extracts what the workflow needs: client name, phone, email, matter title, and whether this is a genuine closure.

Works with both Clio and generic practice management systems — detects the format automatically.

Validity check: proceeds only when (1) matter status is 'Closed' and (2) a client phone number is present. Clio fires 'matter.updated' on every status change — this step filters to final closure only so no premature review requests are sent to active clients.

Output: matter_id, matter_title, client_name, client_phone, client_email, closed_at, source, is_valid_closure, received_at.

nodes.name: If Matter Closure Valid
nodes.notes:
Decides whether to proceed — only genuine matter closures with a client phone number trigger the review request.

Clio fires 'matter.updated' on any status change, not just closure. Without this guard, a matter moved from Pending to Open would trigger a review request for an active client.

Yes (matter Closed + client phone present) → proceed to the 48-hour wait.
No (non-closure update, or no phone number) → route to Skip.

nodes.name: Skip Invalid Matter Closure
nodes.notes: Safely ends the workflow for non-closure matter updates and events missing a client phone number. No messages sent. Visible in execution history for confirmation.

nodes.name: Build Review Request Data
nodes.notes:
Prepares the review request message and calculates the 48-hour send time.

Reads from n8n Variables (project → Variables tab):
  FIRM_NAME — your law firm name as it appears in texts
  FIRM_EMAIL — intake email (firm notifications go here)
  FIRM_FROM_EMAIL — notification sender address; must be authorised by your SMTP account
  FIRM_TWILIO_NUMBER — your Twilio number in +1XXXXXXXXXX format (the same number clients text back)
  FIRM_GOOGLE_REVIEW_URL — Google Business Profile → Home → 'Get more reviews' → copy the short URL
  FIRM_FEEDBACK_URL — a Google Form, Typeform, or your website contact page

Path B (Read Client Reply) reads the same variables — set once in project → Variables tab, used by both paths.

The 48-hour wait is calculated from when this node runs (when the matter-close event arrives).

nodes.name: Wait 48 Hours Before Request
nodes.notes:
Pauses the workflow for 48 hours — the research-backed sweet spot for review requests in professional services. Clients who receive a request too soon feel pressured; too late and they've forgotten the details. 48 hours is the window when the outcome is fresh but the stress of the legal matter has faded.

The execution sits suspended in your n8n database during this wait — no server resources consumed.

Self-hosted n8n: must use Postgres as the database (not SQLite) for long waits to survive a restart. n8n Cloud handles this automatically.

nodes.name: Execute Review Compliance Check
nodes.notes:
Calls the Bar-Compliance Guardrail before sending the review request SMS.

Checks whether the client has opted out. If not, attaches the TCPA opt-out instruction (already present in rating_sms — guardrail detects this and skips duplication). Logs the attempt to your compliance audit sheet regardless of outcome.

The guardrail workflow ID is read from the GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID n8n Variable (project → Variables tab). Set it to the numeric ID from the Bar-Compliance Guardrail workflow URL: .../workflow/WORKFLOW_ID.

Returns: { approved, message_out, recipient, suppression_reason } plus all upstream fields.

nodes.name: If Approved for Rating Request
nodes.notes:
Routes based on the compliance guardrail's decision.

Yes (approved: true) → sends the rating request SMS to the client.
No (approved: false) → client is on the opt-out list; message suppressed. The guardrail has already logged the suppression to your compliance sheet.

nodes.name: Skip Opted-Out Contact Rating
nodes.notes: Client is on the opt-out list — no review request sent. The Bar-Compliance Guardrail has logged the suppression to your audit sheet. No further action needed.

nodes.name: Send SMS Rating Request
nodes.notes:
Sends the review request text to the client 48 hours after matter close.

Message example: 'Hi Jane, it was our honor to serve you at Smith Law Firm. How was your experience? Reply 1–5 (1 = poor, 5 = excellent). Reply STOP to opt out.'

The message comes from the compliance guardrail (message_out) — the opt-out instruction is already in the original text; the guardrail verified it and logged the send.

Credential to wire: project → Credentials tab → New → search Twilio → enter Account SID and Auth Token from console.twilio.com → save as 'Twilio account'.

When the client replies, Twilio fires the inbound message webhook to the Path B URL, which routes their rating. If Twilio returns an error here, the workflow continues to the firm notification so your team is still informed and can follow up manually.

nodes.name: Notify Firm Review Sent
nodes.notes:
Emails your firm a confirmation that the review request was sent, with the matter details and a reminder of how reply routing works.

Informational only — no action needed unless a feedback alert follows.

Credential: project → Credentials tab → New → search SMTP → enter your email provider's settings → save as 'Email account (SMTP)'. Supports Gmail, Outlook 365, Zoho Mail, and any SMTP provider.

All values (client name, matter title, etc.) are read from the Set Up Review Request node — not the immediate input — because the Twilio response from the previous step has a different data shape than the matter details.

parameters.subject: =Review request sent — {{ $('Build Review Request Data').item.json.matter_title }}

nodes.name: When Review Reply Received Webhook
nodes.notes:
Receives the client's inbound reply SMS from Twilio.

Copy the Production URL from this node and paste it into your Twilio number's messaging webhook:
  Twilio Console → Phone Numbers → Manage → Active Numbers → click your number → Messaging Configuration → 'When a message comes in' → Webhook → paste this URL → HTTP POST → Save

This tells Twilio to POST every inbound SMS to this workflow for rating-based routing. The Valid rating? guard handles non-rating messages (opt-outs, questions, unrelated texts) gracefully.

Important: this replaces any existing incoming-message webhook on that Twilio number. If you already route inbound SMS elsewhere, coordinate with your existing setup before saving.

nodes.name: Parse Client Review Reply
nodes.notes:
Parses the client's SMS reply and prepares the routing decision and response messages.

Reads from n8n Variables (project → Variables tab) — the same variables set for Path A (Set Up Review Request). No separate configuration needed here.

Rating logic:
  4 or 5 → happy path → client receives your Google Reviews link
  1, 2, or 3 → unhappy path → client receives private feedback form + firm gets an alert

The parser is tolerant of extra text: '5 stars!' and '5 very happy' both read as 5. Only the leading digit is used.

Opt-out handling: STOP, UNSUBSCRIBE, CANCEL, END, QUIT are filtered here — Twilio also handles STOP independently by suppressing future texts to that number.

Output: from_number, rating, is_happy, is_valid_rating, all firm config, and pre-built SMS text for each routing path.

nodes.name: If Client Rating Valid
nodes.notes:
Checks whether the client's reply is a valid 1–5 rating before routing.

Twilio fires this webhook for ALL inbound messages to your number — including opt-out replies (STOP), questions, and unrelated texts. Without this guard, a non-rating message would incorrectly trigger a Google Reviews link or a feedback alert.

Yes (1–5, not an opt-out) → proceed to happy/unhappy routing.
No (opt-out keyword, non-numeric, or unrelated text) → route to Skip.

nodes.name: Skip Non-Rating Reply
nodes.notes: Safely ends the workflow for non-rating replies (opt-outs, questions, unrelated texts). No Google Reviews link or feedback form sent. Visible in execution history for auditing.

nodes.name: If Client Is Satisfied
nodes.notes:
Routes the client based on their rating — this is the rating gate that protects your Google profile.

4 or 5 stars (Yes) → happy path → compliance check → sends Google Reviews link. Only clients with a genuinely positive experience reach your public Google profile.

1, 2, or 3 stars (No) → unhappy path → compliance check → sends private feedback form + alerts your firm. Concerned clients go to a private channel where you can address the issue before it becomes a public negative review.

nodes.name: Execute Google Link Compliance
nodes.notes:
Calls the Bar-Compliance Guardrail before sending the Google Reviews link.

Checks whether the client has opted out. If not, attaches 'Reply STOP to opt out.' to the Google Reviews message and logs the send to your audit sheet.

The guardrail workflow ID is read from the GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID n8n Variable (project → Variables tab).

nodes.name: If Approved for Google Link
nodes.notes:
Routes based on the compliance guardrail's decision for the 4–5 star happy path.

Yes (approved) → sends Google Reviews link.
No (opted out) → suppressed; guardrail has logged the suppression.

nodes.name: Skip Opted-Out Google Link
nodes.notes: Client rated 4–5 stars but is on the opt-out list — Google Reviews link not sent. The Bar-Compliance Guardrail has logged the suppression to your audit sheet.

nodes.name: Send SMS Google Review Link
nodes.notes:
Sends the Google Reviews link to clients who rated 4 or 5 stars.

Message (with STOP instruction appended by the compliance guardrail): 'Thank you! We're so glad you had a great experience at Smith Law Firm. Would you mind leaving a quick Google review? It truly helps: [link]\n\nReply STOP to opt out.'

This is the rating gate: only clients with a positive experience reach this step and your public Google profile.

nodes.name: Execute Feedback Form Compliance
nodes.notes:
Calls the Bar-Compliance Guardrail before sending the private feedback form link.

Checks whether the client has opted out. If not, attaches 'Reply STOP to opt out.' to the feedback form message and logs the send to your audit sheet.

The guardrail workflow ID is read from the GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID n8n Variable (project → Variables tab).

nodes.name: If Approved for Feedback Form
nodes.notes:
Routes based on the compliance guardrail's decision for the 1–3 star unhappy path.

Yes (approved) → sends private feedback form link + alerts your firm.
No (opted out) → suppressed; guardrail has logged the suppression. No feedback link sent and no firm alert triggered.

nodes.name: Skip Opted-Out Feedback Form
nodes.notes: Client rated 1–3 stars but is on the opt-out list — private feedback form not sent and firm alert suppressed. The Bar-Compliance Guardrail has logged the suppression to your audit sheet.

nodes.name: Send SMS Feedback Form Link
nodes.notes:
Sends the private feedback form link to clients who rated 1, 2, or 3 stars.

Message (with STOP instruction appended by the compliance guardrail): 'Thank you for letting us know. We take every concern seriously and want to make this right. Please share your feedback privately here: [link]\n\nReply STOP to opt out.'

The private path keeps concerns off your public Google profile and gives your team a chance to address the issue — often converting an unhappy client into a loyal one. The next step alerts your firm immediately.

nodes.name: Notify Firm Unhappy Client Feedback
nodes.notes:
Emails your firm immediately when a client rates 1–3 stars.

The alert includes: the client's phone number (so you can call them directly), their rating, their raw reply text, and the private feedback form URL you sent them.

The 24-hour follow-up recommendation is intentional — research shows that firms who address a complaint directly within 24 hours resolve the concern the majority of the time and virtually eliminate the risk of a public negative review.

Uses the same 'Email account (SMTP)' credential as Path A.

parameters.subject: =Client feedback alert — {{ $('Parse Client Review Reply').item.json.rating }}/5 rating from {{ $('Parse Client Review Reply').item.json.from_number }}

description: Sends an SMS review request 48 hours after matter close — the sweet spot when clients remember the outcome but stress has faded. Clients who rate 4–5 stars receive a Google Reviews link; clients who rate 1–3 receive a private feedback form and trigger an internal alert. Replaces Clio Reputation and Birdeye ($49–$250+/mo). Two webhook triggers: matter-close (Path A) and client reply routing (Path B). Opt-out checking and TCPA disclaimer are handled by the Bar-Compliance Guardrail sub-workflow before every client message.
